Call Sheet

Game-plan & play-calling builder · v15.7 RPO Concept Engine
Generating base playbook
Building the saved book, checking formations, and preparing the Build List. You will be taken there automatically.

Playbook Builder

Start with a source-backed offensive system template or choose Custom. Then set the book size and tune the real controls: personnel, formations, run/pass band, motion, and core concepts.

1

Choose your starting point

Templates build a system from a coach profile. Custom ignores templates and uses only your sliders.

2

Pick offensive system

System template is now the top-level identity. The old broad identity selector and tempo/aggression control are retired.

Changing the template updates the system plan. Click Apply system preset to snap the sliders to that profile, or adjust sliders manually.
3

Set book size and discipline

Discipline controls how hard the chosen profile pulls the sliders toward its preset. Custom mode sets discipline to zero.

How many plays to carry into the active book. 5 plays per selected formation is the floor, not the target.
Current: 75%

Set the % of the book for each grouping, including 30 personnel for Flexbone / 3-back option structures. Drag to adjust — total auto-normalises to 100%.

Total: 100%
Strict mode means 0% 11, 10, Flexbone-style personnel, etc. will not leak into generation or live suggestions.
Flow: choose template/custom → set size/discipline → tune personnel and formations → set run/pass and concepts → generate.

Custom mode: system scoring is off. Formation and concept sliders become the main controls.

These are hard rails. If a family is set to 0%, that formation family is removed from generation and live suggestions.

Total: 100%
Formation identity matters.
The app now weights formation family as heavily as concept. Set Flexbone to 0% and a Paul Johnson book will try to find option football outside Flexbone instead of forcing Flexbone back into the book.

v15: formations must also carry enough of the full offensive menu to be selected. A formation is not just a container — it needs a playable package.

Recognizes wide vs condensed spacing, trips/3-man surfaces, quads/4-man surfaces, TE/wing surfaces, nub/unbalanced, 2-back, and 3-back structures. These align with the selected system preset.

Total: 100%
Surface tells the app what the formation is doing.
Example: Wake/Briles should bias wide splits and 3-man surfaces. Shanahan/RVO should bias condensed TE/wing surfaces. DeBesse wants 2/3-back gun or pistol. Flexbone systems want 3-back under-center structure.

Current: 25%. A formation must contain this much of the selected offense's core menu to be included.

The split your book is built around — and the band the in-game caller keeps you inside. System presets can set this automatically; drag either handle to set min and max run %.

Run 45–60% Pass 40–55%

Tell the app what you actually want to major in. These seed the base book, starting call weights, and in-game guardrails. With high System discipline, the selected offensive system carries more weight than broad/default sliders.

Shifts & Motions

Boost motion, shift & disguise looks — jet, orbit, wing, y-off, flex, stack, bunch, nasty, over, unbalanced. Higher = more pre-snap movement in both the base book and in-game suggestions.

35/100 Moderate motion
Static — defense reads alignment Heavy disguise — force late checks

Saved Base Playbook

This is the stable book saved on this device. Download it to keep a backup — upload it on any device or after a wipe to restore everything instantly.

📂
Drop a playbook backup (.json) here or click to browse
Restores playbook, corrections, philosophy, and all game-plan data

Playbook Source

Choose whether the active base playbook is generated from the full database, built from an uploaded team playbook, or designed fully custom in the app. Scout, Script, In-Game, Coach Brain, and Play IQ all use the active base playbook only.

Loading source…
Upload a team/playbook CSV on the Data tab, then choose whether to use it exactly or generate a smaller Coach Brain book from that source. Use blank custom if you want to design the whole book manually.

Add play to book

Add any formation and play directly to your saved book without regenerating. Plays added here are flagged as manual additions and persist through reloads.

Audibles

Four in-formation checks per formation.

Playbook Build List

Sorted by formation — cycle through each formation to build your call sheet. Copy or download the full list as CSV.

FormationPersPlayPlay IntentCore ConceptSituations

Opponent Pre-Game Scout

Set the game-plan direction and what you expect from this defense. That seeds your in-game reads, then builds an opening script — each call designed to confirm a read and feed your in-game adjustments.

Generate and save a base playbook first — the opening script is built from your saved book.

Game situation

Defensive scouting report

What you expect pre-snap. These become tentative reads (dashed) until the opening script confirms them.

Established defensive reads

Shared live with the In-Game caller. Dashed = expected from scouting, solid green = confirmed by a script result. Adjust any of these as the game tells you more.

Opening script

Run top-to-bottom. Log the defense's reaction on each to lock in a read.

Two-minute script

Pre-planned sequence for clock-pressure situations. Adapts to in-game concept weights — better-performing concepts are promoted.

Adaptive In-Game Logic

Log how each concept performs. Working concepts get boosted (capped). Overused concepts get trimmed. A concept that keeps failing the same way gets cut — and its built-in answers get promoted.

Weights start from your saved base playbook.

How to use In-Game

Do not regenerate the playbook during the game. Generate or import the base book once before kickoff. In-Game calls from that saved book.

Game-day flow: choose field position → choose down/distance → tap Suggest 3 plays for the next snap → pick one → run it → log Good / No Harm / Bad. Repeat each snap. Use Reset in-game tracking only when starting a new game or if you want to clear live memory.

Run / pass balance

Live call mix vs your target band (gold). The caller nudges back inside when you drift.

Run 0%
Pass 0%

Active reads

From your scout + script. These bias every suggestion. Edit on the Scout tab as the game evolves.

Game management brain

Live score/clock context. This now changes the call score, suppresses bad clock calls, and changes 3rd-down thinking in four-down mode.

Mode: Normal. Suggestions use standard down/distance rules.
Coach Brain Adjustment

What did the last snap teach us?

Log a play with diagnosis and the app will explain what to protect, cut, and promote.
Drive Sequencing Brain

What did we show, what did they reveal, and what is the answer?

After you log a play, this tracks the picture, defensive tells, and pending answers. The next-call engine now boosts same-picture constraints and punishes blind repeats.

Next call

Pick a play, run it, log the result — tracking updates immediately.
1st & 10 · balanced

Concept board

Live weights, calls, and success rate. ▲ boost · ▼ trim are applied automatically as you log.

Play Corrections

Fix incorrect core concept, play intent, or personnel on any play. Families are now hidden legacy data; the app uses the fuller play-intent categories for display, diagnostics, and live suggestions.

Batch edit — 0 selected plays
FormationPersPlay Core ConceptPlay IntentMotCorrected
Click any cell in the Core Concept, Play Intent, or Pers column to edit it inline. Changes save instantly. After correcting, regenerate your base playbook to apply them.

Formation & Play Intent Tags

The app auto-tags each play from its formation name, play name, core concept, personnel, motion, and hidden source-bucket data when available. These tags now include Play IQ details — route group, read type, timing, coverage fit, protection stress, hot answer, and install confidence. Correct any wrong tag here — your edits are saved and will override the auto-tagger.

FormationPlayConceptSurfaceMotion purpose Run schemePass structureSeriesConflictStress RouteReadTimingBeatsProtectionHotInstallEdited
These tags drive the diagnosis panel, in-game scoring, sequencing, and the opening-script question tree. For example: motion-purpose tags trigger motion-response questions; pass-IQ tags tell the caller whether a concept is good vs man, zone, pressure, match, or needs time.

Upload / Import Play Database

Upload a CSV or JSON play file. The importer now separates offensive plays, defensive plays, and unknown rows. Only offensive plays are used by the offensive Coach Brain caller; defensive plays are saved separately for a future defensive call-sheet module. Required fields: formation, play. Optional fields: concept, personnel/pers, formation_slug, play_slug, play_url. Legacy source-bucket fields are accepted but hidden after import.

📂
Drop CSV or JSON here or click to browse
Accepts .csv or .json · full game databases are split into offense / defense / unknown
